tasks.title: Scaffold + ship piece #1 on mywildlifecam under new framework
tasks.description: DONE 2026-05-16 evening. Shipped as buying guide 'Best Trail Cameras for Backyard Wildlife' at mywildlifecam.com/buyers-guides/best-trail-cameras-for-backyard-wildlife. 3 picks: Spypoint Flex-M (cellular), Stealth Cam DS4K Ultimate (image quality), Wosports H-41 (budget). Ray wrote Bottom Line. Spec-verification caught fictional Wosports H7 before publish. Voice review surfaced 2 doctrine evolutions: em dashes banned, defensive audience exclusions banned. Framework end-to-end validated.

tasks.title: Scaffold + ship piece #2 on mywildlifecam

Lessons folded into future scaffolding.

tasks.title: Wire up CF Pages GitHub auto-deploy (5 sites)
tasks.description: NEW TOP-OF-QUEUE 2026-05-16. The 2026-05-12 bootstrap created the 5 CF Pages projects via direct wrangler upload and never connected them to GitHub. Pushes to main don't auto-deploy. Silent failure mode caught during piece-#3 verification: pieces 'ship to git' but stay invisible on live sites. Immediate fix landed: scripts/deploy.ps1 wraps build+deploy. Permanent fix: connect each of 5 projects to GitHub via CF dashboard so main pushes auto-build+deploy. Walkthrough at docs/cf-pages-github-setup.md with exact build config.
tasks.estimate: 30-60 min total dashboard work (5 sites × 5-10 min each)

subtasks.title: Open dash.cloudflare.com → Pages → affkit-mywildlifecam → Settings → Builds & deployments
Connect to Git (or fall back to 'create new project from Git' per walkthrough's Option 2b if retrofit isn't supported)
Paste build config: command='pnpm install && pnpm --filter mywildlifecam build', output='sites/mywildlifecam/dist', NODE_VERSION=20, NPM_FLAGS=--version
Verify first build succeeds in CF dashboard
Test trigger: make trivial commit, push, watch CF auto-deploy fire
Repeat for affkit-detailerpicks
Repeat for affkit-fussybean
Repeat for affkit-starteraquarium
Repeat for affkit-gameovergear
Verify via wrangler pages project list — all 5 should show Git Provider: GitHub

tasks.notes: Critical because: every future piece silently fails to go live until manually deployed via scripts/deploy.ps1. Walkthrough at docs/cf-pages-github-setup.md handles the pnpm-monorepo config + the fallback path if direct-upload projects can't be retrofitted with Git. After completion, scripts/deploy.ps1 stays useful for hotfixes.

tasks.title: Brainstorm askbigchew integration strategy (migrate vs stay-separate)
tasks.description: Strategic question surfaced 2026-05-16 mid-MVP execution: askbigchew is a real affiliate site, Ray actually owns a bulldog and would USE Big Chew products. The 2026-05-15 voice doctrine assumes 'no hands-on' — but askbigchew is the one site where Ray IS hands-on. Open questions: (1) Should askbigchew migrate from its Next.js+MDX repo into this Astro monorepo as site #6 properly? (2) Does askbigchew get a different voice doctrine that allows hands-on claims since Ray actually owns the products? (3) Hero/satellite tier reshuffle — is bulldog the new hero now that Ray has actual product experience? (4) Does the universal anatomy still hold or does askbigchew need a variant?

subtasks.title: Run ce-brainstorm against the 4 strategic questions above
Decide: migrate to Astro monorepo, or stay separate?
If migrate: plan the Next.js → Astro content migration; if stay-separate: lock the operational pattern (shared link-cloaker + DNS, separate templates + content frameworks)
Decide voice-doctrine variant for askbigchew (hands-on allowed?) and document the decision
Decide hero/satellite tiering (is bulldog the new hero?)

tasks.notes: NOT a blocker for piece #1 on mywildlifecam. Surface for a future session when there's headspace for product-strategy thinking. Current state: askbigchew documented in CLAUDE.md + README as site #6 with separate-repo + separate-stack pattern.

tasks.title: Apply for Amazon Associates under mywildlifecam.com
tasks.description: UNBLOCKED 2026-05-16. Submit Amazon Associates application using mywildlifecam.com as the apex. The 2026-05-15 brainstorm deferred this until 2-3 pieces were live to avoid burning the 180-day-3-sales clock on an empty site. 3 pieces are now live on mywildlifecam. Time to apply.

subtasks.title: Wait until 2-3 pieces are live on mywildlifecam
Submit application at affiliate-program.amazon.com (primary Store ID: mywildlifecam; list all 6 site URLs)
Wait for approval email (1-3 days)
Add tracking tag to scripts/add-link.ps1 default calls per site
Create per-site tracking tags in Associates dashboard (mywildlifecam-20, detailerpicks-20, etc.)
Backfill tags into existing KV entries

tasks.notes: Blocked by first-review (need 2-3 pieces live before applying). Strategic decision locked 2026-05-15.

tasks.title: Migrate askbigchew DNS to Cloudflare
tasks.description: Walk-through doc at docs/askbigchew-cloudflare-migration.html. Brings the 6th sister site under the same Cloudflare umbrella as the other 5 (Pages + link-cloaker Worker).

subtasks.title: Open docs/askbigchew-cloudflare-migration.html and follow the click-by-click
Add askbigchew to CF Pages
Update apex DNS at Namecheap to CF nameservers
Verify HTTP 200 + SSL

tasks.notes: Standalone task — independent of the content-framework strategy question (askbigchew-strategy). DNS migration can happen regardless of whether askbigchew eventually migrates into this monorepo or stays separate.

tasks.title: Enable Cloudflare R2 + apply for Amazon PA-API
tasks.description: Two-step toward product images. (a) Enable R2 in the CF dashboard for hero image hosting. (b) Apply for PA-API (gated on 3 qualifying Associates sales in 180 days — chicken-and-egg with the Associates application).

Wait until 3 Associates sales accumulated
